sub_sections.title: Project's pathways towards impact
sub_sections.content: MAESTRO's outputs provide direct, traceable pathways to both call expected outcomes and five wider expected impacts. For EO-01, OUT-1 (Neuro-symbolic planning framework for LLM-based agents, D2-02, M36), OUT-2 (Adaptive tri-store memory architecture, D3-02, M30), and OUT-9 (External Tool and API Orchestration Layer, D2-02/D8-01) advance AI agent autonomy, robustness, and reliability; for EO-02, OUT-3 (Formally verified multi-agent coordination protocol suite, D4-01/D4-02) delivers innovative multi-agent protocols with formal convergence guarantees. Against the five call expected impacts: three TRL-5 sector demonstrators — OUT-4 (Clinical decision support agent system, D5-02, M42), OUT-5 (Manufacturing process optimisation agent system, D6-02, M42), and OUT-6 (Logistics and supply chain transfer demonstrator, D7-02, M42) — advance the Apply AI Strategy across healthcare, advanced manufacturing, and logistics with cross-sector transferability to in-vehicle autonomous driving evidenced in D7-02 (EI-01); EDIH partnerships under WP9 target ≥3 hospital networks, ≥5 manufacturing SMEs, and ≥10 European SMEs/startups (EI-02); the open-source MAESTRO framework (D9-02, M36) and AI-on-demand contributions (D8-02, M42) strengthen European AI research infrastructure targeting ≥20 publications (EI-03); CERIA's standardisation submission to ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42 or IEEE based on D4-01 advances European strategic autonomy in multi-agent AI norms (EI-04, status: Inferred from topic destination placement — no explicit call scope statement confirmed); the neuro-symbolic planning engine, adaptive memory, and tool orchestration layer enable economically deployable AI agents across data analytics, coding, and domain-specific workflows, with the open-source framework targeting ≥500 GitHub stars within 6 months of M36 release (EI-05). Principal target groups: European AI researchers; hospital networks and health IT vendors; manufacturing and logistics SMEs; European AI startups; standardisation bodies; EU AI Act policy officials. Key barriers and mitigations: EU AI Act clinical deployment timelines are mitigated by auditable reasoning traces in D5-02 and NIHS clinical expert validation; EDIH capacity constraints are mitigated by proactive partnership agreements under WP9 T9-01 from M24.

sub_sections.title: Measures to maximise impact - Dissemination, exploitation and communication
sub_sections.content: Dissemination: MAESTRO disseminates results through ≥20 peer-reviewed publications in top AI venues (NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, AAMAS, AAAI) from M12 (ATU, BIIS, CERIA, FIIT) with ≥3 invited keynotes under WP9 T9-03; the MAESTRO benchmark suite dataset (D8-02, FIIT, M42) and evaluation framework (D8-01, M12) are released as open data on the AI-on-demand platform targeting adoption by ≥5 external research groups; all publications are Plan S-compliant CC-BY on Zenodo and institutional repositories; a Data Management Plan (D1-02, M6) governs FAIR compliance for all project data including de-identified clinical datasets from WP5. Exploitation: NIHS leads EDIH-mediated adoption planning for ≥3 European hospital networks by M48 (D5-02); ISIA transfers the manufacturing demonstrator (D6-02, M42) to ≥5 manufacturing SMEs via ≥2 EDIHs and EIT Manufacturing; ELI establishes a confirmed logistics integration roadmap by M48 (D7-02); BAL manages the open-source MAESTRO framework release (D9-02, M36) targeting ≥10 European SMEs/startups and ≥500 GitHub stars within 6 months; CERIA submits D4-01 as a formal standardisation proposal to ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42 or IEEE by M48 (KPI-08); WP9 T9-02 coordinates all exploitation and IP management activities with outcomes documented in D9-03 (M48). Communication: a project website and public communication plan are established from M1 under BAL (WP9 T9-03); an EU AI Act multi-agent transparency alignment report (CERIA, M48) addresses policy audiences; EDIH networks serve as the primary industrial communication channel. IPR: an open-core Apache 2.0 strategy covers the framework, benchmark suite, and protocol specifications; sector-specific clinical, manufacturing, and logistics demonstrator configurations may be licensed commercially or protected as trade secrets by NIHS, ISIA, and ELI respectively; BAL maintains the consortium IP register. Post-project sustainability rests on open-source community governance (D9-02), operational embedding in commercial environments, and normative anchoring through standardisation of D4-01.

claim_statuses.claim_summary: All five call expected impacts (EI-01 to EI-05) covered by impact pathways in impact_architecture.json
EI-04 (EU strategic autonomy) is inferred from topic destination placement, not confirmed call scope text
TRL-5 demonstrators (OUT-4, OUT-5, OUT-6) grounded in WP5/D5-02, WP6/D6-02, WP7/D7-02
Core technical outcomes (OUT-1, OUT-2, OUT-3) address call expected outcomes EO-01 and EO-02
KPI targets (≥3 hospital networks, ≥15% defect reduction, ≥500 GitHub stars) traceable to Tier 3 impacts and impact_architecture KPIs
Dissemination plan with publications, benchmarks, and AI-on-demand contributions present and substantive
Exploitation plan with EDIH-mediated adoption, SME technology transfer, and logistics integration roadmap present
IPR strategy (Apache 2.0 open-core; sector-specific commercial licensing by NIHS, ISIA, ELI) defined
Post-project sustainability via open-source governance, operational embedding, and standardisation documented
Standardisation submission (D4-01) to ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42 or IEEE traceable to IMP-6 and KPI-08
